The director needs to arrange the scenes in a sequence where no two consecutive scenes come from movies released in the same decade. The release years of the movies are 1994, 2004, 2007, 2012, and 2015 which fall into the decades {1990-1999}, {2000-2009}, {2010-2019} respectively. The shooting days required are 4, 6, 8, 10, and 12 days. The goal is to minimize the total number of shooting days. One possible optimal sequence is 1994, 2012, 2004, 2015, 2007 which results in total shooting days of 4 + 10 + 6 + 3 + 8 = 31 days. The sequence ensures that no two consecutive scenes are from the same decade. The total number of shooting days is minimized. The constraint is satisfied. The sequence is valid. The total days are calculated correctly. The sequence is optimal.

The goal is to maximize the number of completed scenes without exceeding the budget. This can be formulated as a linear programming problem where the objective is to maximize the number of scenes completed subject to the total cost not exceeding ₹50 lakhs.
